title: rubus rosaefolius
intro: Rubus rosaefolius, also known as the rose-leaved raspberry or wild rose raspberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly as a treatment for digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery. The leaves and roots of Rubus rosaefolius contain tannins, flavonoids, and anthraquinones, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Rubus rosaefolius can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, or used in tincture form, to support digestive health and overall well-being.
